notes: Batch-A: 9 panic + 3 telemetry flagged, 18 billing UI translated. Wait — recount: PRD said 9 panic + 3 telemetry, but candidates file contains only 7 panic + 2 telemetry literals for features_page.rs (verified by re-reading candidates). The remaining 2 entries in features_page.rs ('1 million' / '10 million' L699/L701) are tagged action_hint=translate in candidates, NOT flag. They are user-facing — inserted into `block_maximum_rows_description` UI text whose wrapper 'Setting the limit above 100k lines may impact performance. Maximum rows supported is {max_rows}.' is already translated to '上限超过 10 万行可能影响性能。最大支持 {max_rows} 行。' Therefore translated as '100 万' / '1000 万' for Chinese readability. Total: 21 translated + 9 flagged = 30. Glossary decisions: (1) `credits` → `积分` (consistent with existing translated `Add-on credits → 附加积分` and recent billing batches; PRD listed both `credit` and `点数` as candidates but `积分` is the prevailing precedent in this file's already-translated entries). (2) Currency literals ($0.00, $200.00, ${:.2}, ${price_dollars:.2}, ${cost_dollars:.2}) kept verbatim — symbols and format codes have no translatable surface. (3) `%b %d at %-I:%M %p` → `%b %d，%-I:%M %p` — preserves all strftime codes (%b, %d, %-I, %M, %p) per spec §4; only 'at' replaced with Chinese full-width comma. (4) `{ADDON_CREDITS_DESCRIPTION} {ADDITIONAL_ADDON_CREDITS_DESCRIPTION_FOR_TEAM}` is a `format!` template binding two const Rust identifiers to other translatable strings; the only literal content is the separating space. Target is identical to source — composition macro pattern. (5) `Buying…` source uses Unicode ellipsis already; per project convention, target also uses `……` (Chinese full-width double ellipsis). (6) `{discount}% off` → `减 {discount}%` (active voice imperative discount badge; placeholder name preserved). (7) `your selected` → `您选定的` — fragment substituted into 'When enabled, auto reload will automatically purchase {auto_reload_amount} credits...' so noun-modifier form is needed. (8) `1 million` / `10 million` translated as `100 万` / `1000 万` (Chinese myriad convention, with space per CJK-ASCII rule).
